Overview

Address
395 MIDDLE ROAD, NANTICOKE, PA 18634
Phone
5707352973
Certified beds
121

112 (93% of beds filled)

For-profit corporation
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1984-04-09
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.22 3.90 3.19 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.39 0.68 0.39 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.81 0.87 0.80 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.02 2.35 2.00 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.21 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.02
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.23
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.99
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
56.2%
annual
RN turnover
81.3%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.38
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.00
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-04 18 11 7 217 1 217
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-06-07 30 5 25 192 1 192
Total weighted health score
210.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (55)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
51 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2025-09-17 2025-09-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-09-17 2025-09-26
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-09-17 2025-09-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2025-08-20 2025-09-26
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-08-20 2025-09-26
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. E 2025-08-20 2025-09-17
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-06-17 2025-06-27
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-04-04 2025-06-17
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-01-02 2025-01-30
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-01-02 2025-01-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-10 2025-01-02
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-10-24 2025-01-02
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. B 2024-06-07 2024-06-25
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2024-06-07 2024-06-25
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-06-07 2024-06-25
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-06-07 2024-06-25
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-06-07 2024-06-25
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-25
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-25
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-25
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-03-28 2024-05-17
0574 The resident has the right to receive notices in a format and a language he or she understands. E 2024-03-28 2024-05-17
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-03-28 2024-05-17
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-03-28 2024-05-17
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-28 2024-05-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-03-28 2024-05-17
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. E 2023-08-08 2023-09-06
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-08-08 2023-09-06
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2023-08-08 2023-09-06
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2023-08-08 2023-09-06
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. D 2023-06-08 2023-08-08
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. E 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. E 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2023-05-05 2023-06-08
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. E 2023-05-05 2023-06-08

Methodology & sources

Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description

Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2025-06-17 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description

Deficiencies 0809 — description Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. 0809.description
